vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; the black pot contains only a single, struggling, etiolated sprout. P3 (Pothos) is present but appears to be the only surviving specimen.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of silver guest)

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil and a plant marker. No biological growth detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Combined: Black pot contains a single, pale, chlorotic sprout. No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf is present, showing signs of light stress (pale coloration). Rabbit anchor is stable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently damp/dark across all pots. No surface cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P2/P4) acting as a soil amendment or pest deterrent. A small electronic component (IC header) has been introduced to the desk surface in later images.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single pale sprout in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: Receipt/paper debris introduced to the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Electronic component (header) appears on the desk.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: The sprout in the black pot shows slight elongation/postural shift, but remains in a state of severe light starvation.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. The lack of light is causing extreme etiolation in the remaining sprout. P1 is effectively a dead zone. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at intervention, but the lack of chlorophyll in the remaining sprout indicates imminent failure.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is suffering from a critical lack of photosynthetic energy. Only one specimen (P3) retains a healthy leaf structure, while the other pots are either barren or contain a single, struggling, chlorotic sprout. The introduction of non-botanical items (electronics, paper) suggests the desk is being used as a workspace, potentially encroaching on the plants' limited light access.
